messages.author: Caribou80
messages.text: Thank you very much for this new version, which adds significant value to the application! Here’s my feedback : Requests for improvements: The ability to automatically add markers created by other users to the template library (perhaps by long-pressing the received message, similar to "reply"?) The ability to communicate on private channels and hashtag channels, and not only private rooms (because it needs a dedicated node to be used). The ability to open a marker on an external GPS application, from the map screen and from the messages screen. Bugs: When creating new markers in the settings, the background of the icon is always gray for the sender and the receiver, even if a different color was selected in the settings. Specific Bugs in Drawings: When sharing drawings, they always appear as dotted lines for the receiver, with a large label saying "unknown." The message received related to a drawing is in raw format, making it unreadable on the chat screen. Sometimes, the delete button for drawings disappears even though drawings are present on the map. Occasionally, when pressing the share button for drawings, nothing happens. No channel proposal, and no message is sent either. (edited)
messages.content_html:
Thank you very much for this new version, which adds significant value to the application! Here’s my feedback : Requests for improvements:
  • The ability to automatically add markers created by other users to the template library (perhaps by long-pressing the received message, similar to "reply"?)
  • The ability to communicate on private channels and hashtag channels, and not only private rooms (because it needs a dedicated node to be used).
  • The ability to open a marker on an external GPS application, from the map screen and from the messages screen.
Bugs:
  • When creating new markers in the settings, the background of the icon is always gray for the sender and the receiver, even if a different color was selected in the settings.
Specific Bugs in Drawings:
  • When sharing drawings, they always appear as dotted lines for the receiver, with a large label saying "unknown."
  • The message received related to a drawing is in raw format, making it unreadable on the chat screen.
  • Sometimes, the delete button for drawings disappears even though drawings are present on the map.
  • Occasionally, when pressing the share button for drawings, nothing happens. No channel proposal, and no message is sent either.
 (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: TonyA1960
messages.text: Wilderness mode will just allow companion devices to repeat, something they do not currently do. Will allow for better message distribution without the need for additional repeaters. Having a central message repository would be good. Everyone would need to log in and Syncronise messages, that way missing data would be discovered. Would however use a lot of bandwidth but I am sure the clever people could figure something out.

messages.author: Gary Mortimer
messages.text: Beyond my pay grade! We used to deploy trackers on elephants and rhinos and our collars receivers would pull all data since the animal walked in range. Commercial vehicle/asset trackers used the same system and would send us any data they might pick up as well. Made the network much bigger. The SAR app might be a starting point to store and then forward to anyone in the group anything they had not heard.

messages.author: dz0ny
messages.text: It’s the same as in the stock app; it’s built around adverts. So you can either increase adverts and set hop = 0, or use a side channel to actually send updates faster (what SAR app does if you enable it). The side channel is specific to the SAR app, and with PR https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1928 , we can do that for a specific “channel” and also allow for relays and make it private. Repeating group data updates is something that is being considered and would probably only be allowed with the “Repeat nearby traffic” option already supported by the hardware, and that would be also private and isolated. It would be somewhat gated to avoid spamming the public mesh. Just a reminder: under special rules, you can use any frequency if permitted by your local governing authority, so this would fall under that use. And in general you don't want to spam any kind of mesh :). (edited)
messages.content_html: It’s the same as in the stock app; it’s built around adverts. So you can either increase adverts and set hop = 0, or use a side channel to actually send updates faster (what SAR app does if you enable it). The side channel is specific to the SAR app, and with PR https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1928, we can do that for a specific “channel” and also allow for relays and make it private. Repeating group data updates is something that is being considered and would probably only be allowed with the “Repeat nearby traffic” option already supported by the hardware, and that would be also private and isolated. It would be somewhat gated to avoid spamming the public mesh. Just a reminder: under special rules, you can use any frequency if permitted by your local governing authority, so this would fall under that use. And in general you don't want to spam any kind of mesh :). (edited)
